sections.text: When you use a website, there are always two sides talking to each other: the client and the server. Understanding the difference between them is one of the most important first steps in web development.

sections.title: What is a Client?
sections.text: The client is the device or app that asks for something. When you open a website on your phone or laptop, your browser is acting as the client. The client sends a request, like saying please send me that page, and then waits for an answer.

sections.title: What is a Server?
sections.text: The server is a computer that waits for requests and answers them. It stores things like web pages, images, and data, and whenever a client asks for something, the server sends it back. A server can answer many clients at the same time, all day and all night.

sections.title: How a Client and Server Talk
items: The client (your browser) sends a request, asking for a page or some data.
The request travels across the internet to the right server.
The server receives the request and decides what to send back.
The server sends a response back to the client.
The client receives the response and shows it to you, like a finished web page.

sections.title: Think of it like a Waiter and a Kitchen
sections.text: You are sitting at a table in a restaurant. You are the client, and you tell the waiter what you want. The waiter takes your order to the kitchen, which is the server. The kitchen prepares the food and the waiter brings it back to you. You never go into the kitchen yourself, you just ask and receive.
One single server can talk to thousands or even millions of clients at the same time, answering each request separately.

terms.definition: The device or app, like your browser, that asks a server for information.
A computer that stores information and sends it back when a client asks for it.
A message the client sends asking for something, like a web page.
The message the server sends back, containing the information the client asked for.
The system where clients ask for things and servers answer them.

questions.text: What is a client in web development?
options: A computer that stores all the websites
The device or app that asks a server for information
questions.explanation: The client is the device or app, usually your browser, that sends a request asking for a web page or other information.

options: It stores information and sends it back when a client asks for it
It is the same thing as a browser
questions.explanation: A server stores things like web pages and data, and sends them back whenever a client sends a request asking for them.

questions.text: In the restaurant analogy, who plays the role of the server?
questions.explanation: The kitchen prepares the food, just like a server prepares and sends back the information that was requested.
